texto: America seemed like a place for honest people. The United States of America. The land of the free and the home of the brave. The most righteous, politically correct and awesomest country in the world. It even says "in god we trust" on the dollar bills. God bless America.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Carlito Sofer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a coragem. O trecho “America seemed like a place for honest people. The United States of America. The land of the free…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que coragem, america, americans pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de americans e usa.

frase_original: America seemed like a place for honest people. The United States of America. The land of the free and the home of the brave. The most righteous, politically correct and awesomest country in the world. It even says "in god we trust" on the dollar bills. God bless America.

texto: Many young athletes joined the gangs instead of aspiring to gold medals in the Olympics. You could easily discern the kind of sport they did by their body shape and injuries. Well-built with a broken nose - a boxer.Broad shoulders with torn ears - a wrestler. Enormous muscles with little to no brain - a bodybuilder. Short with broad shoulders and a quadratic head - a weightlifter.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Carlito Sofer sobre temas ligados a inteligencia. A afirmação “Many young athletes joined the gangs instead of aspiring to gold medals in the Olympics. You coul…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a inteligencia, communism, entrepreneurship-business, útil em momentos de dia-chuvoso e viagem.

texto: I remember the teacher telling us that Ukraine was part of the Soviet Union, alongside Russia. “The Soviet Union is the largest and most glorious empire that the world has ever seen,” the teacher lectured. “We’re all proud comrades. We’re all like brothers. We’re so lucky to be part of the greatest nation that has ever existed. We love our country and our country loves us like a mother loves her children.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Carlito Sofer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I remember the teacher telling us that Ukraine was part of the Soviet Union, alongside Russia. “T…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, communism, empiricism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e empiricism.
